title: Companies House Free Company Data Product
short_description: Monthly snapshot of basic data for all live UK companies including registration details
provider: Monthly bulk CSV snapshot of all active companies on the UK register, including registered address, company type, status, SIC codes, and key dates. Updated within 5 working days of month end. Contains approximately 5 million active companies split across multiple ZIP files. Widely used for business intelligence, compliance screening, and market analysis. Does not include dissolved companies or detailed officer/shareholder data; those require the API or separate products.
access_method: Single monthly snapshot — updated each month end
tags: CompanyName|string|Registered company name; CompanyNumber|string|Unique 8-digit company identifier; RegAddress.AddressLine1|string|First line of registered address; RegAddress.PostCode|string|Postcode of registered office; CompanyCategory|string|Legal type e.g. Private Limited Company; CompanyStatus|string|Current status e.g. Active/Liquidation; IncorporationDate|date|Date company was incorporated DD/MM/YYYY; SICCode.SicText_1|string|Primary SIC code and description

priority: companies, register, corporate, UK, incorporation, SIC codes, business, addresses, compliance
column22: Data split across approximately 6 ZIP files. Does not include dissolved companies. Field specification PDF at https://resources.companieshouse.gov.uk/toolsToHelp/pdf/freeDataProductDataset.pdf — approximately 55 fields total. Companion Companies House API provides real-time data on officers and filings. Record count fluctuates monthly.

title: HM Land Registry Price Paid Data
short_description: All residential property sales in England and Wales from January 1995 to present with price and address details.
long_description: Complete record of every residential property transaction registered with HMLR since January 1995, covering over 28 million sales. Includes sale price, completion date, full address, property type, freehold/leasehold tenure, and whether new build. Updated monthly. Approximately 1 million new transactions added per year. Does not cover Scotland, Northern Ireland, or commercial property. One of the most-used open datasets in the UK — foundational for housing market analysis, automated valuations, and economic research.
access_method: Free bulk download — full file (5.3 GB CSV) or annual splits
schema_fields: transaction_unique_identifier|string|Auto-generated unique reference for each sale; price|integer|Sale price in GBP stated on transfer deed; date_of_transfer|date|Date sale completed YYYY-MM-DD; postcode|string|Property postcode at time of sale; property_type|string|D=Detached S=Semi-Detached T=Terraced F=Flat O=Other; old_new|string|Y=newly built property N=established residential building; duration|string|F=Freehold L=Leasehold; paon|string|Primary address object name or number; street|string|Street name; town_city|string|Town or city; county|string|County name; ppd_category_type|string|A=standard residential B=additional transaction type
column56: property, housing, real estate, sales, prices, land registry, residential, England, Wales, transactions
column59: No column headers in raw file by default — select 'with headers' option on download page. Full dataset is 5.3 GB CSV. Linked data version available via SPARQL at landregistry.data.gov.uk. Does not include commercial property or Scottish/Northern Irish transactions. Category Type B transactions include repossessions and buy-to-lets.

title: Charity Commission Register of Charities
short_description: Full register of all charities in England and Wales including income
source_url: Daily extract of all registered and recently removed charities in England and Wales, covering approximately 170,000 organisations. Data is split across 15 linked tables covering charity details, registration history, names, trustee details, area of operation, classification by theme and beneficiary, and annual financials. Income and expenditure data available for charities with income over £500,000. Updated daily. Widely used for due diligence, grant-making, and civil society research. Some financial detail limited for smaller charities.
update_frequency: Charity Commission for England and Wales
sample_rows: All currently and recently registered charities; historical from founding
priority: regno|integer|Unique charity registration number; subno|integer|Subsidiary number 0 for main charity; name|string|Registered charity name; orgtype|string|Organisation type code; income|integer|Latest reported annual income GBP; expenditure|integer|Latest reported annual expenditure GBP; registration_date|date|Date first registered with Charity Commission; removal_date|date|Date removed from register if applicable
column21: charities, nonprofits, third sector, England, Wales, income, trustees, registration, voluntary sector
column24: Data provided as 15 linked relational tables — requires joining on regno and subno keys. Detailed financials only for charities with income above £500,000 (Part B returns). Format changed from BCP to JSON/TSV in 2021. Similar data available via the Charity Commission API at api.charitycommission.gov.uk.

title: Ofsted State-funded School Inspections and Outcomes
short_description: Monthly dataset of all Ofsted inspection outcomes for state-funded schools in England with judgements across key areas.
long_description: Monthly management information dataset covering all Ofsted inspection outcomes for approximately 22,000 state-funded schools in England including primary, secondary, special schools, and alternative provision. Contains inspection dates, overall effectiveness judgements on a 1–4 scale (Outstanding/Good/Requires Improvement/Inadequate), and sub-judgements for quality of education, behaviour and attitudes, personal development, and leadership and management. Covers inspections from September 2005 to present. Published within weeks of each month end. Key resource for local authority benchmarking and school improvement planning.
provider: Office for Standards in Education Children's Services and Skills (Ofsted)
sample_rows: URN|string|Unique Reference Number identifying the school; EstablishmentName|string|School name; OverallEffectiveness|integer|Overall Ofsted judgement 1=Outstanding 2=Good 3=Requires Improvement 4=Inadequate; QualityOfEducation|integer|Quality of education sub-judgement 1-4; LeadershipAndManagement|integer|Leadership and management sub-judgement 1-4; InspectionDate|date|Date of most recent inspection DD/MM/YYYY; SchoolType|string|Type of school e.g. Academy Converter LA Maintained; Postcode|string|School postcode

access_type: schools, Ofsted, inspection, education, England, outstanding, primary, secondary, academy, judgement
column21: Separate datasets available for early years, further education, and children's social care. Five-Year Ofsted Inspection Data (ODS format) also published annually. URN links to Get Information About Schools (GIAS) register. Field descriptions in 'Key' tab of each download file.

title: ONS Mid-Year Population Estimates England and Wales
short_description: Annual mid-year population estimates for England and Wales by local authority
provider: Annual official population estimates for England and Wales published by the Office for National Statistics, covering all local authorities and London boroughs by single year of age and sex. Includes components of population change — births, deaths, and internal and international migration. Available from 1971 to present. Published annually approximately 18 months after the reference year. Used by NHS, local authorities, and central government for resource allocation, planning, and demographic analysis. Data is available via the ONS API for automated access.
access_method: 000 rows per annual release (local authority × age × sex)
tags: Free bulk download; Free API via developer.ons.gov.uk
access_type: geography_code|string|ONS geography code e.g. E09000001; geography_name|string|Local authority or region name; year|integer|Reference mid-year e.g. 2023; age|integer|Single year of age 0 to 90+; sex|string|Male/Female/Persons; population|integer|Estimated mid-year population count
priority: E09000001,City of London,2023,25,Male,1450; E09000002,Barking and Dagenham,2023,0,Persons,4234; E92000001,England,2023,45,Female,2143800
notes: population, demographics, local authority, London, age, sex, migration, births, deaths, England, Wales
column23: ONS API available at developer.ons.gov.uk — allows filtering by geography and time period. 2021 Census-based estimates rebased from 2022 release onwards. Sub-national projections published separately. Also available broken down by ethnicity in separate releases.

title: UK House Price Index
short_description: Monthly house price index and average prices for 441 UK areas from 1995 broken down by property type and buyer status.
long_description: Official UK House Price Index published jointly by HM Land Registry, ONS, Registers of Scotland, and NISRA. Covers average prices and index values (base 100 = January 2015) for over 441 geographic areas including UK regions, counties, local authorities, and London boroughs. Includes breakdowns by property type (detached, semi-detached, terraced, flat) and buyer type (first-time buyer, existing owner-occupier, cash buyer, mortgage buyer). Data from 1995 for England and Wales; 2004 for Scotland; 2005 for Northern Ireland. Published monthly with approximately 2-month lag. Queryable via SPARQL or CSV download.
date_coverage: January 1995 to present (England and Wales); 2004 Scotland; 2005 Northern Ireland
access_method: Free bulk download; Free API (SPARQL endpoint)
schema_fields: refArea|string|ONS geography code for the area; refPeriod|date|Reference month YYYY-MM-DD; averagePrice|decimal|Mean house price GBP; index|decimal|Index value base 100 January 2015; percentageChange|decimal|Monthly percentage change; percentageAnnualChange|decimal|Annual percentage change; salesVolume|integer|Number of sales transactions in period; averagePriceDetached|decimal|Mean price for detached properties; averagePriceFlat|decimal|Mean price for flats and maisonettes
tags: house prices, property, index, housing market, UK, London, mortgage, real estate, first time buyer
notes: SPARQL endpoint at landregistry.data.gov.uk/landregistry/query. Also published as GOV.UK statistical release monthly. Note: sales volume data has 3-4 month additional lag. Separate dataset for Price Paid individual transactions. London-specific view available on London Datastore.

title: FCA Financial Services Register
short_description: Live register of all FCA-authorised firms and individuals in UK financial services including permissions and regulatory status.
long_description: The Financial Services Register is the definitive public record of all firms and individuals authorised or registered by the Financial Conduct Authority, covering approximately 50,000 authorised firms and 1.5 million approved individuals. Includes firm permissions (regulated activities), contact details, trading names, appointed representatives, and current regulatory status. Updated in real-time. Public search and API are free with registration; bulk structured extract requires paid subscription via the Register Extract Service (RES), provided by Spectrum Data Management. Critical for compliance screening, due diligence, and financial sector analysis.
licence: UNKNOWN — check publisher terms (public search free; bulk extract via commercial Register Extract Service)
format: REST API; CSV bulk extract via paid Register Extract Service
schema_fields: Free API — registration required at register.fca.org.uk; Paid subscription for bulk extract

RES handbook at https://www.fca.org.uk/publication/documents/register-extract-handbook.pdf. Commercial licence required for RES — email ODL team before purchasing. Similar data for PRA-regulated firms available from Bank of England.

title: Met Office HadUK-Grid Gridded Climate Observations
short_description: 1km gridded UK climate observations from 1836 covering temperature
provider: and frost days — monthly and daily.
source_url: HadUK-Grid is the Met Office's official gridded dataset of UK climate observations, interpolated from the surface station network onto a 1km × 1km grid. Available at 1km, 5km, 12km, and 25km resolutions with daily, monthly, seasonal, and annual aggregations. Variables include maximum temperature, minimum temperature, mean temperature, total rainfall, sunshine hours, days of snow lying, and frost days. Some variables available from 1836; rainfall from 1891; temperature from 1960. Accessed via the CEDA Archive. Used for climate trend analysis, environmental modelling, planning, and research.
access_type: Free bulk download — free registration required via CEDA Archive (ceda.ac.uk)
priority: longitude|float|Longitude of 1km grid cell centre degrees; latitude|float|Latitude of 1km grid cell centre degrees; time|date|Observation date or month YYYY-MM-DD; tas|float|Mean air temperature degrees Celsius; tasmax|float|Maximum air temperature degrees Celsius; tasmin|float|Minimum air temperature degrees Celsius; rainfall|float|Total rainfall millimetres; sun|float|Sunshine duration hours; groundfrost|float|Number of days with ground frost
column21: climate, weather, temperature, rainfall, sunshine, UK, environment, meteorology, grid, historical
column24: Format: NetCDF — requires specialist tooling (Python xarray/netCDF4 or CDO). Not directly queryable as CSV without conversion. CEDA registration is free but requires an account. Older versions archived; current version is HadUK-Grid v1.2. Also available via the CEDA THREDDS server for subset extraction. Consider pairing with Met Office DataPoint API for near-real-time station data.
